records.ety: [OF. estrangler, F. trangler, L. strangulare, Gr. <?/, <?/, fr. <?/ a halter; and perhaps akin to E. string, n. Cf. Strain, String.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Strangled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Strangling (?).]

defs: To compress the windpipe of (a person or animal) until death results from stoppage of respiration; to choke to death by compressing the throat, as with the hand or a rope.
quotes: Our Saxon ancestors compelled the adulteress to strangle herself.

defs: To stifle, choke, or suffocate in any manner.
quotes: Shall I not then be stifled in the vault, . . . And there die strangled ere my Romeo comes?

defs: To hinder from appearance; to stifle; to suppress.
